questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
Move out of this lane safely
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
You may turn in the arrow's direction
questions.explanation: At a green light, you may go if the way is clear. At a green arrow, you may go in the direction of the arrow if the way is clear. If you are turning without a green arrow, you must yield the right-of-way to vehicles coming from the other direction and pedestrians in the intersection. Be sure to check for less visible vehicles such as motorcycles, bicycles, and mopeds. If a traffic light changes from red to green while a pedestrian is in the street, allow the pedestrian to cross the street before turning.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for deer crossing the road
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: Never drive in a lane marked with a red X signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Go if the way is clear
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Move out of this lane safely
questions.explanation: These signals mean that you should move out of the lane as soon as safely possible.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to drive in a lane marked with a green arrow signal.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may drive in this lane
You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
Go if the way is clear
questions.explanation: You are permitted to enter in a lane marked with a one-way or two-way arrow only to turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: You may turn in the arrow's direction
Move out of this lane safely
You may drive in this lane
Stop for pedestrians when the beacon is flashing or solid red
questions.explanation: PHBs appear over intersections without stoplights and alert drivers when pedestrians are at a crosswalk. One yellow light at the bottom flashes when a pedestrian activates the PHB. Next, the yellow light turns solid to alert drivers to prepare to stop. Then, the top two red lights on the PHB turn solid while a walk signal appears at the crosswalk, and drivers must stop. Lastly, while the walk signal counts down for the pedestrians, the PHB's two red lights alternate flashing, telling the driver that if the crosswalk is now clear, they may proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: School zone or school crossing ahead
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: This eight-sided shape always means stop. You must come to a complete stop at the sign, stop line, pedestrian crosswalk or curb. Wait for any vehicle or pedestrian to clear the way. At some intersections you'll find a sign beneath the stop sign that reads "All Way" or "4 Way." At these intersections all vehicles on all roads leading into the intersection must stop. If you get to the intersection at the same time as other vehicles, the driver on the left must yield to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Gives the law or directional information
questions.explanation: You must slow down as you come to the intersection. Be prepared to stop. Let any vehicles, pedestrians or bicyclists safely pass before you

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
Gives the law or directional information
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: Vertical signs generally give instructions or tell you the law. Horizontal signs may give directions or information.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
questions.explanation: These signs warn you of special conditions or hazards ahead. Slow down and drive with caution. Be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield — slow down and give the right-of-way
Warns of conditions or hazards ahead
School zone or school crossing ahead
questions.explanation: This five-sided shape marks school zones and warns you about school crossings. Two signs may be used together to show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
questions.explanation: These signs tell you the maximum legal speed that you may drive on the road where the sign is posted when weather conditions are good. Some roads have electronic speed limit signs that change based on weather or traffic conditions. During rain, snow and ice, you may receive a ticket for driving too fast for the conditions even if you are driving at or less than the posted speed limit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: These signs mean you cannot drive in that direction. If you drive past these signs you are going in the wrong direction and could get into a head-on crash with vehicles headed your way. Immediately slow down, pull over, and cautiously turn around.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Left turns are against the law. In Virginia, U-turns are considered as two left turns and are illegal if this sign is posted.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: Right turns are illegal. Do not make a right turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: U-turns are illegal. Do not make a U-turn when you see this sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Maximum legal speed in good conditions
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: You may not turn on the red light. Wait for the signal to turn green.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: No passing — start of a no-passing zone
Do not drive in this direction
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Turning left on green must yield to oncoming traffic
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: This sign is used with a traffic signal. It tells you that the traffic turning left at a green light does not have the right-of-way and must yield to traffic coming from the other direction. Stop and look for oncoming traffic, then proceed with caution.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Keep right of the island, median, or barrier
Do not drive in this direction
questions.explanation: A traffic island, median or barrier is ahead. Keep to the side indicated by the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
questions.explanation: These signs are used where turns are required or where special turning movements are permitted for specific lanes. Traffic in the lane must turn in the direction of the arrow.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Do not turn while the light is red
Do not drive in this direction
Lane reserved for buses and carpools (HOV)
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: These signs indicate lanes reserved for buses and vehicles with a driver and one or more passengers as specified on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Parking reserved for disabled permit holders
Traffic flows only in the direction of the arrow
Tells which turns are required or allowed from a lane
Do not turn while the light is red
questions.explanation: Parking spaces marked with these signs are reserved for people with disabled parking permits.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign indicates the maximum safe speed for a highway exit.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A stop or yield sign is ahead
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: A stop sign or yield sign is ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Traffic signals are ahead. Slow down and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign marks the beginning of a no passing zone. You may not pass cars ahead of you in your lane, even if the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Two lanes are merging into one
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: Two lanes of traffic moving in the same direction are about to become one. Drivers in both lanes are responsible for merging safely.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
questions.explanation: The right lane ends soon. Drivers in the right lane must merge left when space opens up. Drivers in the left lane should allow other vehicles to merge smoothly.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
A stop or yield sign is ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: The highway ahead is split into two separate roadways by a median or divider and each roadway is one-way. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
The right lane ends ahead; merge left
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The highway ahead no longer has a median or divider. Traffic goes in both directions. Keep right.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: When pavement is wet, reduce your speed. Do not brake hard or change direction suddenly. Increase the distance between your car and the one ahead of you.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Two lanes are merging into one
questions.explanation: The overpass ahead has a low clearance. Do not proceed if your vehicle is taller than the height shown on the sign.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: A steep grade is ahead. Check your brakes.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for deer crossing the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: Deer cross the roadway in this area. Slow down, be alert and be ready to stop.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
The road ahead is divided by a median
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Slow down and don't use the horn. State law requires motorists to pass with at least three feet of clearance when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Steep downgrade ahead — check your brakes
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: Regularly travel in this area. Be ready to slow down or stop. Only pass when the way is clear.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
A stop or yield sign is ahead
questions.explanation: Watch for people who are walking, riding bicycles or other devices entering a crosswalk or crossing your path. Slow down and be prepared to stop. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the crosswalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Bicycles regularly cross or ride beside traffic in this area. Drive with caution. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the bike crossing.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
questions.explanation: Watch out for children crossing the street or playing. Be ready to slow down or stop at school zones and surrounding areas. Obey the speed limit and signals from crossing guards. A second sign with an arrow may show the actual location of the sidewalk.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Watch for children — school zone or crossing
questions.explanation: Slow down. Open joints on bridges or ramps could cause a motorcyclist to lose control of the motorcycle.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
Traffic signals are ahead — be ready to stop
questions.explanation: This sign is used when a joint across lanes creates a bump or is wide enough to cause loss of traction in wet weather.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: An intersection is ahead. Be alert for vehicles entering the road on which you are traveling.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
Open joints on a bridge or ramp ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: You must bear either right or left ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
questions.explanation: The roadway you are traveling on ends ahead at a stop sign. You must turn right or left after yielding to oncoming traffic and pedestrians.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right then left turns ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a circular intersection with an island in the center is ahead. Also called traffic circles, these intersections may have one or more lanes. Entering traffic must yield the right-of-way to traffic already in the circle and travel in a counter clockwise direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A right curve with a side road joining ahead
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right and a side road joins from the left within the curve. Be alert for vehicles entering the roadway you are traveling on.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for a sharp right turn in the road ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road ahead is divided by a median
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Slow down and be prepared for the road ahead to turn sharply right, then left.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Watch for children — school zone or crossing
Watch for horse-drawn buggies on the road
Recommended safe speed for the road or exit ahead
The road curves right, then left
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right, then left. Slow down.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Right curve ahead — slow to the posted safe speed
Low overpass ahead — do not enter if too tall
Watch for bicycles crossing or sharing the road
questions.explanation: The road ahead curves right. Slow down to the safe speed indicated.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Your road ends ahead at a T intersection
Road is slippery when wet — slow down
questions.explanation: The road ahead winds with a series of turns or curves. On all curves, slow down for better control.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Sharp right turn ahead — slow down
Low-ground railroad crossing — long vehicles may get stuck
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: A steep slope where the railroad tracks cross the road may cause the bottom of low vehicles to get caught or drag on the tracks.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The divided highway ends; expect two-way traffic
Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
A joint creates a bump across the lane — slow down
questions.explanation: Advance warning signs are placed before a railroad crossing. These signs warn you to look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: The road curves right, then left
Watch for deer crossing the road
Watch for tractors and slow farm equipment
questions.explanation: This sign is a warning of a railroad crossing. Look, listen, slow down and be prepared to stop for trains or any vehicles using the rails. Trains may be approaching from either direction. If there is more than one track, trains may be approaching from either direction on either track.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with flashing lights — stop when flashing
The road splits ahead — bear right or left
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Flashing lights may be used with crossbuck signs. Always stop when the light begins to flash and be alert for approaching trains. Do not proceed until all trains or any other vehicles using the rails have passed, the tracks are clear, and the lights are no longer flashing. Be especially alert at multi- track crossings because a second train could be approaching from the opposite direction.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Railroad crossing with gates — never drive around a lowered gate
Sharp right then left turns ahead
questions.explanation: Gates are used with flashing light signals at some crossings. Stop when the lights begin to flash and before the gate lowers. Remain stopped until the gates are raised and the lights stop flashing. Do not attempt to drive around the lowered gate.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs are used when certain road conditions, such as loose gravel or road construction, affect the roadway surface and create potentially difficult conditions for motorists, especially motorcyclists.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
questions.explanation: These signs indicate a change in the traffic pattern or route ahead. Slow down. Unusual or potentially dangerous conditions are ahead.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Large flashing arrow boards or flashing message signs in work zones direct drivers to proceed into different traffic lanes and inform them that part of the road ahead is closed.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
questions.explanation: Flaggers are highway workers who normally wear orange or yellow vests, or yellow-green shirts or jackets. They use STOP/SLOW paddles or red flags to stop or direct traffic through the work zone, and to let other workers or construction vehicles cross the road.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
Rough road, bump, or uneven lanes ahead
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: This sign indicates that automated photo enforcement is in place for speeding in a work zone. Always obey the posted speed limit in a work zone.

questions.prompt: What does this sign mean?
choices: Barricades, cones, and barriers guide traffic in work zones
Flashing arrow board — move out of the closed lane
A flagger is directing traffic — obey their signals
questions.explanation: Barricades, vertical signs, concrete barriers, drums and cones are the most common devices used to guide drivers safely through work zones. When driving near the devices, keep your vehicle in the middle of the lane and obey the posted speed limit. As you leave the work zone, stay in your lane and maintain your speed. Don't change lanes until you are completely clear of the work zone.

questions.prompt: What does a steady red traffic light mean?
choices: Slow down and proceed with caution
Come to a complete stop and wait
Stop only if other vehicles are present
Yield to traffic on your left
questions.explanation: A steady red light requires you to stop completely and remain stopped until the light turns green.

questions.prompt: What is the shape of a stop sign?
questions.explanation: A stop sign is always a red eight-sided octagon, a shape used only for stopping.

questions.prompt: A flashing red traffic signal should be treated the same as which sign?
questions.explanation: A flashing red light means stop completely, then proceed when it is safe, just like a stop sign.

questions.prompt: What does a steady yellow traffic light mean?
choices: The signal is about to turn red, so prepare to stop
Speed up to clear the intersection
questions.explanation: A steady yellow light warns that the signal is about to turn red and you should stop if you can do so safely.

questions.prompt: What color and shape identifies a warning sign for hazards ahead?
questions.explanation: Yellow diamond-shaped signs warn drivers of upcoming hazards or changes in road conditions.

questions.prompt: What does a solid yellow line on your side of the centerline mean?
choices: Passing is not allowed on your side
questions.explanation: A solid yellow line on your side means you may not cross it to pass.

questions.prompt: What does an orange traffic sign usually indicate?
questions.explanation: Orange signs mark construction and maintenance work zones where you should slow down and watch for workers.

questions.prompt: What does a green arrow signal mean?
choices: You may proceed in the direction of the arrow after yielding
Stop and wait for a red light
The arrow is a warning only
questions.explanation: A green arrow gives you a protected right to proceed in that direction after yielding to any pedestrians or vehicles still in the intersection.

questions.prompt: What do broken white lines on a roadway separate?
choices: Lanes of traffic moving in the same direction
questions.explanation: Broken white lines separate lanes traveling the same direction and may be crossed when changing lanes safely.

questions.prompt: When a traffic signal is completely dark due to a power failure, you should:
choices: Treat the intersection as an all-way stop
questions.explanation: When signals are dark, treat the intersection as a four-way stop and proceed in turn.

questions.prompt: At an intersection with no signs or signals, who has the right-of-way when two vehicles arrive at the same time?
questions.explanation: When two vehicles reach an uncontrolled intersection at the same time, the driver on the left yields to the driver on the right.

questions.prompt: When you want to turn left at an intersection, you must yield to:
choices: Oncoming traffic that is close enough to be a hazard
No one if you have a green light
questions.explanation: A left-turning driver must yield to oncoming vehicles that are close enough to pose a danger.

questions.prompt: What must you do when an emergency vehicle approaches with lights and siren on?
choices: Pull to the right edge of the road and stop
Speed up to get out of the way
questions.explanation: You must yield to emergency vehicles by pulling over to the right and stopping until they pass.

questions.prompt: Who has the right-of-way at a marked crosswalk?
questions.explanation: Drivers must always yield to pedestrians who are crossing in a crosswalk.

questions.prompt: How far before a turn should you signal in Idaho?
choices: At least 100 feet before the turn
questions.explanation: You should signal continuously for at least the last 100 feet before turning to warn other drivers.

questions.prompt: When entering a roundabout, you must:
choices: Yield to traffic already in the roundabout
Stop and wait for all traffic to clear
Yield only to vehicles on your left exiting
questions.explanation: Drivers entering a roundabout must yield to vehicles already circulating inside it.

questions.prompt: When making a right turn, you should turn from:
choices: The lane closest to the right curb
questions.explanation: Right turns should begin and end as close as practical to the right-hand curb or edge of the road.

questions.prompt: When you are entering a road from a driveway or alley, you must:
choices: Yield to vehicles and pedestrians already on the road
Proceed because you have the right-of-way
questions.explanation: A driver entering from a driveway or alley must yield to traffic and pedestrians already on the roadway.

questions.prompt: At a four-way stop, who proceeds first?
choices: The vehicle that arrived and stopped first
questions.explanation: At an all-way stop, the first vehicle to come to a complete stop has the right to go first.

questions.prompt: In Idaho, a bicyclist approaching a stop sign may legally:
choices: Treat the stop sign as a yield sign
Only stop if a car is present in front
questions.explanation: Under Idaho law, bicyclists may treat a stop sign as a yield, so drivers should watch for cyclists slowing rather than fully stopping.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, what is the default speed limit in an Idaho residential or business district?
questions.explanation: Idaho's default speed limit in residential, business, and urban districts is 35 mph unless a different limit is posted.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, what is the maximum default speed limit on an Idaho interstate highway?
questions.explanation: Idaho's default interstate speed limit is 75 mph, and it may be raised to 80 mph on certain stretches after a traffic study.

questions.prompt: What does Idaho's basic speed rule require?
choices: Driving no faster than is reasonable and prudent for conditions
Always driving the posted limit exactly
Driving 5 mph below the limit at all times
Matching the speed of surrounding traffic
questions.explanation: The basic speed rule means you must adjust your speed to conditions, even if that is below the posted limit.

questions.prompt: What is a safe minimum following distance under good conditions?
choices: At least three to four seconds
questions.explanation: A following distance of three to four seconds gives you time to react and stop safely under normal conditions.

questions.prompt: In rain, snow, or heavy traffic, you should:
choices: Slow down even if below the posted limit
Increase your speed to clear the area
questions.explanation: Posted limits are set for ideal conditions, so you must slow down when weather or traffic reduces safety.

questions.prompt: Unless otherwise posted, what is the default speed limit on an Idaho state highway?
questions.explanation: Idaho's default speed limit on state highways is 65 mph unless a different limit is posted.

questions.prompt: How does higher speed affect your vehicle's stopping distance?
choices: Stopping distance only matters in rain
questions.explanation: The faster you drive, the greater the distance needed to stop, so you must leave more space ahead.

questions.prompt: When should you increase your following distance beyond the normal amount?
choices: On wet, icy, or low-visibility roads
Never, three seconds is always enough
questions.explanation: Slippery roads and poor visibility require a larger following distance because stopping takes longer.

questions.prompt: If you are being tailgated, the safest response is to:
choices: Increase your following distance to the vehicle ahead
Brake hard to warn the tailgater
Speed up well over the limit
questions.explanation: Adding space in front gives you more room to slow gradually, reducing the chance of being rear-ended.

questions.prompt: Driving too slowly compared to surrounding traffic can be dangerous because it:
choices: Can block traffic flow and cause crashes
Has no effect on other drivers
questions.explanation: Driving far below the flow of traffic can frustrate and surprise other drivers, leading to unsafe passing and collisions.

questions.prompt: On a multilane highway, which lane should slower traffic use?
questions.explanation: Slower traffic should keep right, leaving the left lane for passing.

questions.prompt: Before changing lanes, what should you do in addition to checking your mirrors?
choices: Look over your shoulder to check the blind spot
questions.explanation: Mirrors have blind spots, so a quick shoulder check confirms no vehicle is beside you before changing lanes.

questions.prompt: You may not pass another vehicle when:
choices: Approaching a hill or curve where you cannot see far enough ahead
The road is straight and clear
A broken yellow line is on your side
questions.explanation: Passing on hills and curves is unsafe because you cannot see oncoming traffic in time.

questions.prompt: After passing a vehicle, when is it safe to return to your lane?
choices: When you can see the passed vehicle in your rearview mirror
As soon as you are alongside it
questions.explanation: Seeing the passed vehicle in your mirror means you have enough room to merge back safely.

questions.prompt: When another vehicle is passing you on a two-lane road, you should:
choices: Maintain your speed or slow slightly
Speed up to prevent the pass
questions.explanation: Keeping a steady speed or slowing slightly lets the passing driver complete the maneuver safely.

questions.prompt: Passing is generally prohibited within how many feet of an intersection or railroad crossing?
questions.explanation: You may not pass within 100 feet of an intersection or railroad crossing because of the danger of cross traffic.

questions.prompt: On a road with a center left-turn lane (two-way), this lane may be used for:
choices: Making left turns from either direction
questions.explanation: A shared center turn lane is only for vehicles preparing to turn left, never for passing or through travel.

questions.prompt: What should you do before merging onto a freeway?
choices: Use the on-ramp to match the speed of traffic and merge into a gap
Stop at the end of the ramp and wait
questions.explanation: Accelerating to traffic speed on the ramp lets you blend smoothly into a gap without disrupting other drivers.

questions.prompt: On a two-lane road, you may pass on the right:
choices: Only in specific situations, such as when a vehicle is turning left
questions.explanation: Passing on the right is allowed only in limited cases, such as when the vehicle ahead is turning left and there is room.

questions.prompt: Idaho's Move Over law requires you to do what when approaching a stopped emergency vehicle with flashing lights?
choices: Slow down below the speed limit and move over a lane when possible
questions.explanation: Idaho's Move Over law requires drivers to reduce speed and change to a non-adjacent lane when safe to protect roadside workers.

questions.prompt: When following a motorcycle, you should:
questions.explanation: Motorcycles can stop quickly and are harder to see, so give them a full lane and extra space.

questions.prompt: Large trucks have large blind spots, which means:
choices: If you cannot see the driver's mirrors, the driver cannot see you
Trucks can always see all around them
You should drive in the blind spot
Blind spots only exist behind the truck
questions.explanation: Trucks have wide blind spots, so staying where you can see the mirrors keeps you visible to the driver.

questions.prompt: When passing a bicyclist, you should:
choices: Slow down and leave a safe distance
Honk loudly to move them over
Speed up to get past quickly
questions.explanation: Bicyclists need extra room, so slow down and leave a safe gap when passing them.

questions.prompt: After passing a large truck, you should not return to its lane until:
choices: You can see the entire front of the truck in your mirror
questions.explanation: Trucks need long stopping distances, so merge back only when you can see the whole truck behind you.

questions.prompt: When you see a school bus stopped with its red lights flashing and stop arm out, you must:
choices: Stop until the lights stop flashing and the arm is withdrawn
questions.explanation: A stopped school bus with flashing red lights means children may be crossing, so you must stop until it is safe.

questions.prompt: On rural Idaho roads, you should watch for:
choices: Faster speed limits than the interstate
questions.explanation: Rural roads often carry slow farm vehicles and may have livestock, so stay alert and be ready to slow down.

questions.prompt: A pedestrian using a white cane or guide dog is:
choices: Likely visually impaired and always has the right-of-way
Required to wait for all traffic
questions.explanation: Pedestrians who are blind or visually impaired have the right-of-way, so you must stop and yield to them.

questions.prompt: Why should you avoid driving alongside a large truck for long periods?
choices: You may be in a blind spot and the truck may need your lane
It is illegal to be near a truck
questions.explanation: Lingering beside a truck keeps you in a blind spot where the driver may not see you if they need to change lanes.

questions.prompt: What is the legal BAC limit for drivers age 21 and older in Idaho?
questions.explanation: Drivers 21 and older are considered legally impaired at a blood alcohol concentration of 0.08 percent or higher.

questions.prompt: Under Idaho's zero-tolerance law, what BAC can lead to a charge for a driver under 21?
questions.explanation: Idaho's zero-tolerance law sets a BAC limit of just 0.02 percent for drivers under 21.

questions.prompt: Under Idaho's hands-free law, which use of a cell phone is allowed while driving?
choices: Holding the phone to read texts
Typing a message at a red light
questions.explanation: Idaho's hands-free law bans holding or manually operating a device but permits hands-free, voice, or single-touch use.

questions.prompt: Which of the following can impair your ability to drive?
choices: Prescription and over-the-counter medicines, not just alcohol
questions.explanation: Many legal medicines can slow reaction time and cause drowsiness, impairing driving just like alcohol.

questions.prompt: The only sure way to reduce your blood alcohol concentration is to:
questions.explanation: Only time lets the body remove alcohol; coffee, food, and showers do not speed up the process.

questions.prompt: What is the safest way to handle your phone while driving?
choices: Put it away and deal with it after you stop
Use it only on quiet roads
questions.explanation: Putting the phone away removes the temptation to look at it and keeps your attention on driving.

questions.prompt: Drivers under 18 in Idaho are subject to which additional rule?
choices: Greater restrictions on using electronic devices while driving
questions.explanation: Idaho places stricter device-use restrictions on drivers under 18 to reduce distracted-driving crashes.

questions.prompt: Besides texting, which is an example of distracted driving?
choices: Eating or adjusting controls while driving
Keeping both hands on the wheel
questions.explanation: Anything that takes your eyes, hands, or mind off driving, like eating or fiddling with controls, is a distraction.

questions.prompt: Driving while drowsy is dangerous because it:
choices: Slows reaction time and can lead to falling asleep
questions.explanation: Fatigue impairs reaction and judgment much like alcohol and can cause a driver to fall asleep at the wheel.

questions.prompt: In Idaho, you must turn on your headlights from sunset to sunrise and whenever visibility is less than how many feet?
questions.explanation: Idaho law requires headlights from sunset to sunrise and any time you cannot see at least 500 feet ahead.

questions.prompt: When driving in fog, you should use:
questions.explanation: Low beams light the road without reflecting back off the fog the way high beams do.

questions.prompt: If your vehicle begins to hydroplane, you should:
choices: Ease off the accelerator and keep the wheel steady
questions.explanation: Easing off the gas lets the tires slow and regain contact with the road without skidding.

questions.prompt: Which part of the road is most likely to freeze first in cold weather?
questions.explanation: Bridges and overpasses lose heat from both sides and freeze before regular roadways.

questions.prompt: On a wet or icy road, you should:
choices: Reduce speed and increase following distance
Follow more closely to see better
questions.explanation: Slippery roads lengthen stopping distance, so slow down and leave extra room to react.

questions.prompt: If your car starts to skid, you should:
choices: Ease off the gas and steer in the direction you want to go
Steer in the opposite direction of the skid
questions.explanation: Gentle steering toward your target and easing off the accelerator helps the tires regain grip.

questions.prompt: Idaho law generally requires you to turn on headlights whenever you turn on your windshield wipers in poor weather. Why?
choices: It improves visibility and helps others see you
questions.explanation: Low visibility during rain or snow makes headlights important so you can see and be seen.

questions.prompt: When traveling Idaho mountain passes in winter, you should carry:
choices: Tire chains or proper winter tires
questions.explanation: Mountain passes can require chains or winter tires to maintain traction in snow and ice.

questions.prompt: If you are caught in heavy rain and visibility is very poor, the safest action may be to:
choices: Pull completely off the road and wait until it improves
Speed up to get through it
questions.explanation: When you cannot see safely, pulling well off the road and waiting is safer than continuing or stopping in traffic.

questions.prompt: How far from a fire hydrant must you park in Idaho?
choices: Right next to it is fine
questions.explanation: Parking within 15 feet of a fire hydrant is prohibited so firefighters can reach it in an emergency.

questions.prompt: When parking facing downhill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
questions.explanation: Turning the wheels toward the curb when facing downhill lets the curb stop the car if it rolls.

questions.prompt: When parking facing uphill next to a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
questions.explanation: Facing uphill, turning the wheels away from the curb lets the tires roll back against it if the car moves.

questions.prompt: Where is parking prohibited?
questions.explanation: Parking in a crosswalk or intersection blocks pedestrians and traffic and is not allowed.

questions.prompt: Before pulling away from a parked position at the curb, you should:
choices: Signal and check for traffic and cyclists
questions.explanation: Signaling and checking mirrors and blind spots ensures you do not pull into oncoming traffic or a cyclist.

questions.prompt: When you park your vehicle on any hill, you should always:
questions.explanation: Always set the parking brake, and turn your wheels appropriately, so the vehicle cannot roll into traffic.

questions.prompt: Parking in a space marked for people with disabilities is allowed only if:
choices: You have a valid disability permit or plate
questions.explanation: Accessible parking spaces may be used only by vehicles displaying a valid disability permit or plate.

questions.prompt: Driving on the paved shoulder to pass slower traffic is:
questions.explanation: The shoulder is not a travel lane, and using it to pass is illegal and dangerous.

questions.prompt: How many questions are on the Idaho written knowledge test?
questions.explanation: The Idaho knowledge test contains 40 questions covering signs, laws, and safe driving.

questions.prompt: What score is required to pass the Idaho written knowledge test?
questions.explanation: You must answer at least 34 of 40 questions correctly, which is 85 percent, to pass.

questions.prompt: Under Idaho's Graduated Driver Licensing program, how many hours of supervised driving practice are required?
choices: At least 50 hours, including at least 10 at night
questions.explanation: Idaho requires at least 50 hours of supervised practice, including a minimum of 10 hours at night.

questions.prompt: Who must accompany a supervised instruction permit holder in Idaho?
choices: A licensed driver at least 21 years old seated beside the permit holder
Any licensed driver of any age
questions.explanation: A permit holder must be supervised by a licensed driver at least 21 years old sitting in the front passenger seat.

questions.prompt: During the first six months after licensing, an Idaho driver under 17 is generally limited to how many passengers under 17 who are not family?
questions.explanation: For the first six months, drivers under 17 may carry no more than one passenger under 17 who is not an immediate family member.

questions.prompt: What tests must you pass to receive an Idaho instruction permit?
choices: A vision test and a written knowledge test
questions.explanation: An instruction permit requires passing both the vision screening and the written knowledge test.

questions.prompt: Idaho's Graduated Driver Licensing (GDL) program is designed to:
choices: Let new drivers gain experience gradually under reduced risk
questions.explanation: The GDL program phases in driving privileges so new drivers build skills under safer, lower-risk conditions.

questions.prompt: A driver under 17 in Idaho must complete which of the following before getting a license?
choices: An approved driver training course and supervised practice
questions.explanation: Teens under 17 must complete approved driver training and the required supervised practice hours before licensing.

questions.prompt: In Idaho, which children must be secured in a child safety restraint?
choices: All children six years of age and younger
questions.explanation: Idaho law requires every child six years old and younger to be properly secured in an appropriate child safety restraint.

questions.prompt: What does Idaho's implied consent law mean?
choices: By driving you agree to chemical testing when lawfully requested
You can always refuse a breath test without penalty
It applies only to commercial drivers
questions.explanation: Under implied consent, driving in Idaho means you agree to alcohol or drug testing when lawfully requested, and refusal carries penalties.

questions.prompt: Who is required to wear a seat belt in Idaho?
questions.explanation: Idaho law requires the driver and front-seat passengers to wear seat belts, and they greatly reduce crash injuries.

questions.prompt: A DUI conviction in Idaho can result in:
choices: License suspension, fines, and possible jail time
A small fee with no record
questions.explanation: A DUI conviction carries serious consequences including suspension, fines, and potential jail time.

questions.prompt: Why is wearing a seat belt important even on short trips?
choices: It is the most effective way to prevent injury or death in a crash
Crashes never happen on short trips
It is only required on highways
It only matters at high speeds
questions.explanation: Most crashes happen close to home, and seat belts are the single most effective protection in a collision.

questions.prompt: Refusing to take a chemical test when lawfully requested in Idaho results in:
choices: Its own penalties under the implied consent law
questions.explanation: Refusing a lawful chemical test triggers separate penalties, including license suspension, under implied consent.

questions.prompt: Accumulating too many traffic violations can lead to:
choices: License suspension and higher insurance costs
questions.explanation: Repeated violations add up on your record and can cause your license to be suspended and your insurance to rise.

questions.prompt: Driving in Idaho without required insurance is:
choices: Only a problem after a crash
questions.explanation: Idaho requires drivers to carry liability insurance, and driving without it leads to fines and license consequences.

questions.prompt: If you are involved in a crash that causes injury or significant property damage, you must:
choices: Stop, exchange information, and report as required
Drive away if you are not at fault
questions.explanation: Leaving the scene of a crash with injury or damage is illegal; you must stop, exchange information, and report it.
